attacking_style: Launches long-range breakaway efforts designed to build substantial time gaps before final climbs, but lacks explosive finishing power to hold off late pursuers in the final kilometer.
team_tactics: Appears to operate with limited team support in breakaway scenarios, relying on solo endurance efforts rather than coordinated team setups.
terrain_preference: Mountain stages with opportunities for early breakaways where time gaps can be established before final climbs; struggles on technical descents and urban circuits requiring sharp handling skills.
vulnerability: Cannot defend large advantages when caught on steep gradients in final kilometer; technical descents and tight urban circuits expose bike handling deficiencies, including crash risk under pressure.
key_patterns: Builds massive time advantages (7+ minutes) but lacks explosive power to finish climbs alone.
Technical handling issues evident: crashes on tight bends and struggles with urban circuits.
Breakaway specialist who fades when pure climbing power required in final kilometer of ascents.
form_trajectory: negative: Both 2017 Grand Tour appearances showed critical weaknesses—crash vulnerability and inability to convert major breakaway advantage
